steps.title: Identify the slope from the data
steps.content:
Reading the scatterplot, the points progress from approximately (1, 25) at the left to (5, 65) at the right. As $x$ increases, $y$ increases consistently, showing a positive slope.

Calculate the slope:
$$\frac{65 - 25}{5 - 1} = \frac{40}{4} = 10$$
Since choices A and C both have slope $-10$ (negative), they cannot model increasing data. This eliminates A and C.

steps.title: Test the remaining models at a data point
steps.content:
Remaining choices B and D both have slope $10$ but differ in their y-intercept: $+14.7$ versus $-14.7$.

Check both at the point $(1, 25)$ from the scatterplot:
  • Choice B: $y = 14.7 + 10(1) = 24.7$ — matches the data ✓
  • Choice D: $y = -14.7 + 10(1) = -4.7$ — does not match ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
